Half-volley from round the wicket and Seales lifts it over mid-on for a boundary. Not a great delivery, but Seales does well to not hit it hard and just time it over the inner circle

22:26 Local Time, 02:26 GMT, 07:56 IST: Shimron Hetmyer does to New Zealand what Mitchell Santner did to West Indies in the fourth ODI, but this time with a whole lot more Caribbean flair. The left-hander stays unbeaten on 69 off 64 as the hosts go over the line with two wickets to spare. West Indies end the series on a high with an impressive chase in the fifth ODI, powered by superb knocks from Sherfane Rutherford and Hetmyer. However, it's New Zealand who take the series 3-2. Remarkably, all five matches have been won by the team batting second. West Indies chased down 267 and 268 in the first and fifth ODIs respectively, but their struggles batting first proved costly. They were bowled out for under 150 twice, while their 188 in the fourth ODI wasn't quite enough as New Zealand edged home by one wicket. West Indies also dropped a crucial catch in the closing stages of that game, and had that opportunity been taken, the series scoreline could well have been different.

Talking about the match, fifties from Will Young, Nick Kelly and Tom Latham powered New Zealand to 268/9. In reply, West Indies openers Ackeem Auguste (34 off 24) and Justin Greaves (24 off 15) got the chase off to a flying start, adding 50 runs in under six overs, but both fell in successive overs. Keacy Carty and Shai Hope then steadied the innings with a 59-run stand, but neither could convert their start into a match-winning knock. It was eventually Rutherford (61 off 58) and Hetmyer who joined forces, adding a crucial 95 runs to put West Indies in a commanding position. However, Rutherford's needless dismissal, stumped attempting an ambitious shot, gave New Zealand a way back into the contest. Gudakesh Motie, Matthew Forde and Alzarri Joseph departed in quick succession, and it looked like West Indies were on the verge of throwing the game away. But Shimron Hetmyer held his nerve, stayed till the end, and made sure the hosts got over the line.

Mitchell Santner collects the trophy before the rest of the New Zealand players join him as they celebrate and pose in front of the cameras. That's it from this five-match ODI series. West Indies are next in action when they host Pakistan for a two-match Test series starting on July 25. As for New Zealand, they don't play again until October, when they welcome India for five T20Is, five ODIs and a two-Test series. That's all from us for now. Until next time, goodbye and cheers!
